Title: Warm temperature suppresses plant systemic acquired resistance by intercepting N-hydroxypipecolic acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pipecolic acid and N-hydroxypipecolic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Pip/NHP branch of systemic acquired resistance metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected local leaves and distal systemic leaves during SAR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central systemic acquired resistance signal that promotes disease resistance and immune preparedness
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates Pip biosynthesis from lysine via aminotransferase reaction leading to formation of the Pip precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Pip precursor leading to pipecolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the warm temperature-suppressed, rate-limiting NHP biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transgenic complementation/overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen induced in local infected leaves and systemic leaves at 23 degrees C; suppressed at 28 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Pst DC3000 infection locally at 1 dpi and systemically at 2 dpi at 23 degrees C; reduced at elevated temperature; restored in 35S::CBP60g and 35S::SARD1 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; Pst DC3000/AvrRpt2 infection; elevated temperature 28 degrees C; intermediate temperatures 24.5 and 26.5 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome meta-analysis using SAR+ genes from Hartmann et al. 2018 interfaced with temperature-regulated transcriptome from Kim et al. 2022; Borealis repository for source data: 10.5683/SP3/72JVY7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD1 expression is induced by pathogen infection during SAR at 23 degrees C but strongly suppressed at warm temperature. 2. Reduced ALD1 expression correlates with lower Pip and NHP accumulation and loss of SAR. 3. Constitutive CBP60g or SARD1 expression restores ALD1 expression under warm conditions, linking ALD1 to thermosensitive systemic immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Pip/NHP accumulation and systemic acquired resistance; warm-temperature suppression of ALD1 contributes to SAR loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of local and systemic tissues; metabolite profiling of Pip and NHP by LC-MS/MS; SAR pathogen growth assays; genetic restoration in 35S::CBP60g and 35S::SARD1 lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato SlALD1 and rapeseed BnaALD1 orthologs also show warm-temperature suppression, indicating conservation across species
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or engineer temperature-resilient ALD1 induction, potentially via upstream regulators such as CBP60g/SARD1, to preserve NHP-mediated immunity under warming climates

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pipecolic acid and N-hydroxypipecolic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final NHP-forming step of the Pip/NHP branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected local leaves and distal systemic leaves during SAR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central SAR metabolite conferring systemic disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates pipecolic acid to produce N-hydroxypipecolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the warm temperature-suppressed, rate-limiting NHP biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transgenic overexpression lines of upstream regulators
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen induced in local and systemic tissues at 23 degrees C; suppressed at 28 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript accumulation after pathogen infection at elevated temperature in local and systemic tissues; restored by constitutive CBP60g or SARD1 expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; elevated temperature 28 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome meta-analysis using prior SAR and temperature datasets; Borealis repository 10.5683/SP3/72JVY7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is a core NHP biosynthetic gene whose pathogen-induced expression is reduced at elevated temperature. 2. Lower FMO1 expression accompanies reduced NHP accumulation and abolished SAR. 3. Constitutive expression of CBP60g or SARD1 restores FMO1 expression under warm conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NHP production and SAR; its warm-temperature downregulation contributes to loss of systemic immunity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite quantification of NHP; SAR pathogen assays; transgenic restoration experiments
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rapeseed BnaFMO1 also shows temperature-sensitive suppression after infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering stable FMO1 induction or its upstream regulators may sustain NHP production and systemic immunity under climate warming

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Local infected leaves and systemic leaves during immune activation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense hormone required for establishment and amplification of systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the SA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in SA biosynthesis, but NHP production is the rate-limiting step emphasized for warm-temperature SAR loss in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced locally and systemically after pathogen challenge at 23 degrees C; suppressed at 28 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Systemic induction after virulent or avirulent primary infection at 23 degrees C but not at 28 degrees C; local induction also reduced at elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; Pst DC3000/AvrRpt2 infection; NHP treatment; Pip treatment; elevated temperature 28 degrees C
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR and LC-MS/MS for SA/SAG; transcriptome meta-analysis from previous datasets; Borealis repository 10.5683/SP3/72JVY7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 expression is induced during local and systemic immune responses at 23 degrees C but suppressed at elevated temperature. 2. This indicates warm-temperature inhibition of SA biosynthesis alongside NHP pathway suppression. 3. Exogenous NHP restores disease resistance at warm temperature without restoring ICS1 or SA levels, showing ICS1 induction is not sufficient or strictly required for NHP-mediated protection under heat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA biosynthesis, but its suppression at warm temperature accompanies SAR loss; NHP can bypass the need for restored ICS1 induction under heat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of local and systemic tissues; LC-MS/MS quantification of SA and SAG after NHP treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly assayed in Results as part of the linked SA-NHP immune metabolic network, although the central metabolite focus of the paper is NHP rather than SA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful in combination with NHP-pathway engineering, but enhancing ICS1 alone may not fully overcome warm-temperature suppression of SAR

Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome meta-analysis of prior SAR and temperature datasets; Borealis repository 10.5683/SP3/72JVY7
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amino acid-derived defense metabolite; plant hormone
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Local infected leaves and systemic tissues during SAR
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NHP and SA promote systemic acquired resistance and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Constitutive or temperature-resilient CBP60g expression is a promising strategy to maintain NHP biosynthesis and SAR under warming climates
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen induced locally and systemically at 23 degrees C; immune induction lost at 28 degrees C
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suppressed by elevated temperature in wild type; constitutively expressed in 35S::CBP60g plants
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CBP60g is functionally redundant with SARD1 but differs in regulatory behavior; its protein is reported to require calmodulin interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of NHP and SA during systemic immunity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator whose warm-temperature downregulation is linked causally to loss of NHP biosynthesis and SAR; constitutive expression rescues the phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; constitutive overexpression in 35S::CBP60g plants; pathogen growth/SAR assays; downstream ALD1/FMO1 expression and Pip measurements
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; elevated temperature 28 degrees C
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of the positive immune amplification network linking NHP and SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ALD1, FMO1, SARD4 and ICS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g transcript induction after pathogen infection is lost at elevated temperature in local and systemic tissues. 2. Constitutive CBP60g expression restores ALD1/FMO1 expression, Pip accumulation, and SAR under warm conditions. 3. Therefore CBP60g is a master regulator of thermosensitive NHP-mediated systemic immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NHP-pathway gene expression and restores warm-temperature disease resistance when constitutively expressed

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SAR Deficient 1; CBP60 family transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive SARD1 expression confers enhanced basal/primed immunity even without SAR induction, which may complicate deployment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome meta-analysis of prior SAR and temperature datasets; Borealis repository 10.5683/SP3/72JVY7
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amino acid-derived defense metabolite; plant hormone
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Local infected tissues and systemic leaves during SAR
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote systemic immunity and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate for engineering heat-resilient immunity, though constitutive expression may create constitutive priming-like phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen induced locally and systemically at 23 degrees C; suppressed at 28 degrees C
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Wild-type induction is lost at elevated temperature; constitutive expression in 35S::SARD1 restores ALD1 and FMO1 expression at warm temperature
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike CBP60g, SARD1 does not require calmodulin and behaves as a later pathogen-responsive regulator; overexpression causes constitutive SAR-like resistance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of NHP and SA in systemic acquired resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator whose temperature-sensitive expression governs NHP-pathway suppression; constitutive SARD1 restores NHP-pathway gene expression under heat
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; temperature; NHP treatment induces SARD1 at both temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; constitutive overexpression in 35S::SARD1; downstream target gene expression assays; SAR phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; elevated temperature 28 degrees C; NHP treatment
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No named complex established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in positive immune amplification linking NHP and SA pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ALD1, FMO1, SARD4 and ICS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 immune induction is suppressed at elevated temperature in local and systemic tissues. 2. Constitutive SARD1 expression restores ALD1 and FMO1 expression under warm conditions. 3. 35S::SARD1 plants show strong constitutive disease resistance, indicating potent control over the SAR metabolic network.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NHP-pathway gene expression and disease resistance; constitutive expression bypasses warm-temperature suppression
